articles.title: All you need to know about Meta’s Instagram, Whatsapp and Facebook subscription plans in India
articles.description: Meta has launched paid subscription plans for WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook in India, offering customisation and convenience features for a monthly fee. WhatsApp Plus costs Rs 79, while Instagram and Facebook Plus are Rs 99 (with an introductory Rs 49 for Instagram). These moves aim to diversify revenue beyond advertising and fund AI investments. The success of these subscriptions in India, where users are price-sensitive, remains to be seen.

articles.title: Daily Pisces Horoscope: Astrologer warns against haste and risk; follow gentle routines
articles.description: Today, Pisces, navigate with care as the stars suggest a tendency to rush and misunderstand situations. Pay special attention while engaging in physical activities or driving to avoid mishaps. In your relationships, seek to preserve peace rather than engage in conflicts. Financially, be deliberate in your choices and prioritize essential expenses to maintain stability, while also being mindful of your health and well-being amid potential stress.

articles.title: Mosyle launches new service to help parents manage Mac and iPad screen time for K-12 devices at home
articles.description: Managing school-issued Macs and iPads has always been a balancing act between IT control and parent visibility when the devices go home. Today, Mosyle has announced Mosyle@Home , aimed at solving this problem. This new platform gives parents and guardians an official way to manage school devices after hours, and it supports school-issued iPads and Macs.

articles.title: Daily Aries Horoscope: Partner support, money caution; focus on studies and health
articles.description: Aries, today is a great day for partnerships, as the Moon encourages teamwork and collaboration. Venus adds a touch of warmth to your daily interactions, highlighting the importance of kind gestures over elaborate plans. Be cautious with your words, particularly towards women, due to Saturn's influence. Expect positive shifts in love and career through cooperative efforts and fresh opportunities. Financially, prioritize sensible savings over risky investments.

articles.title: iPhone 17 set to break a record no flagship has touched in 15 years
articles.description: When Apple released the iPhone 17 in September, the timer started for the new flagship model breaking a record set 15 years ago. The record will take more than a year to break, but based on current rumors, the iPhone 17 will be the first to steal the crown.

articles.title: India rewrites telecom rulebook, replacing licence contracts with statutory authorisations
articles.description: New government rules replace the telecom industry's contractual licensing with a unilateral statutory model. Operators will now receive authorization after meeting requirements, with future government directives becoming binding terms. Violations will be treated as statutory breaches, managed internally, and can be appealed to the TDSAT. This shift aims to streamline regulation, though telcos expressed concerns about investment uncertainty.

articles.title: Here's when Apple is starting mass production of the iPhone Ultra
articles.description: Recently there's been some controversy about the iPhone Ultra's release window, with a report claiming it was delayed, which was then immediately refuted by another source, and then another one. Today we get one more 'confirmation' that the iPhone Ultra will be unveiled as scheduled, in September, alongside the iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max. The issues relating to the hinge have allegedly been resolved, and the mass production of the iPhone Ultra will be starting in late July. This information comes from a report in Korean media, purportedly originating from "Apple supply chain...

articles.description: Chinas exports of electric vehicles hit a record $9.2 billion in May, rising nearly 50% year-on-year, as global demand for cleaner transport strengthened amid higher oil prices linked to the Iran conflict.

articles.title: Watch: ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok: can India afford AI’s thirst for water?
articles.description: India is rapidly emerging as a global data centre hub, thanks to lower construction costs, tax incentives and growing digital demand. But data centres are also highly water-intensive, raising concerns in a country already facing severe water stress. With India's data centre capacity expected to nearly quadruple by 2030, experts are questioning how the country will balance economic growth with sustainable water use. The debate has intensified as industries such as data centres, nuclear energy and food processing expand in regions already struggling with water scarcity. #DataCentres #WaterCrisis #India

articles.title: Learn about Apple's history with Prime Day book deals at up to 65% off
articles.description: Learn more about the history of Apple and the legacy of Steve Jobs in these biographies, which are discounted by up to 65% during Amazon's Prime Day sales . Apple biographies are on sale at Amazon. We at AppleInsider chiefly deal with the current state of Apple and associated news stories. But it's always good to look back at the company's history to get a more rounded view of its activities. What follows is a selection of biographies, dealing with the company's history through good times and bad, as well as that of Steve Jobs. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: 'It's culture and it's rare': Microsoft employee explains why she still enjoys going to office after 5 years
articles.description: The discussion began after Deepika Koul posted a video on Instagram reflecting on her professional journey at Microsoft. In the clip, she explained that her enthusiasm for work is not driven by the company's reputation or the building itself, but by the people she interacts with and the environment she experiences each day.

articles.title: DMRC begins work on Central Vista metro corridor at Central Secretariat station
articles.description: Construction has begun on the Central Secretariat metro station, a key part of the new Central Vista corridor. This nearly 10-km underground stretch will significantly improve connectivity to government hubs, integrating with existing metro lines. Union Minister Manohar Lal highlighted the project's potential to save commuting time and enhance convenience for thousands of employees and visitors, solidifying the DMRC's status as a world-class transport system.

articles.title: Rishabh Pant traded to Delhi Capitals; Kuldeep Yadav moves to LSG
articles.description: Rishabh Pant has been traded from Lucknow Super Giants to Delhi Capitals, with Kuldeep Yadav moving the other way. DC captain Axar Patel welcomed his friend back, promising him full freedom and no extra pressure at his 'home' franchise.

articles.title: Queen Maxima lauds India's financial inclusion, digital progress
articles.description: Queen Maxima of the Netherlands is on a 3-day visit to India in her UN capacity. She praised India's digital public infrastructure and progress in financial inclusion, stating the next challenge is leveraging these to improve people's lives.
